title: 3.3 and 3.4 practice
description: Practice what you learned in lessons 3.3 and 3.4.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why are mutations, slight imperfections made when copying DNA, important to adaptation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: While most mutations have no effect, some are helpful and increase the chance of survival and reproduction.
All mutations create new, helpful traits that increase the chance of survival and reproduction.
Mutations lead to the mass dying off of individuals with bad genes.
Over generations, mutated genes become more common in the population over time.

assessment_items.question_content:
**On which islands did Charles Darwin make some of his most important observations?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What theory from Darwin suggests that species adapt to their local conditions through variations between generations and “survival of the fittest”?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**How did Darwin explain the different beaks on finch species throughout the Galapagos Islands?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The beaks showed adaptation to the specific conditions on the island.
The birds were introduced on the islands by humans.
The beaks reflected the different micro-climates on the island.
The birds weren’t all finches, though they may have appeared similar.
